originalTitle: Desperate for food drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
title: The Amazing Gingerbread Tree Helps Our Friends in Kenya!
content:
Hello, little adventurers! It's your friend, Reporter Roar, here with some news from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we are going on a special journey to a country called Kenya. We will learn about a special tree and kind people who are helping others.

Big Problem: No Rain, No Food

Imagine looking up at the sky every day. You wish and wish for rain. But the clouds stay empty. This is what has been happening in parts of Kenya. When rain does not fall for a very, very long time, we call it a drought. A drought means the ground gets very dry. Plants cannot grow. Farmers cannot grow yummy food like maize or beans. Animals don't have fresh grass to eat. When there is no food, people get very, very hungry. Their tummies feel empty. Many people in Kenya, and in nearby countries like Ethiopia and Somalia, are finding it hard to get enough food. A kind group called Oxfam says that many, many people – about 26 million – are very hungry. That's like all the kids in many, many schools! *Did you know?* Water is super important for everything! We need it to drink, plants need it to grow, and animals need it to live. Without rain, life becomes very difficult.

Meet Kenya: A Beautiful Place

Kenya is a beautiful country right here in Africa. It has big, tall mountains and wide, flat plains. You can see amazing animals there, like lions, elephants, and giraffes! The people of Kenya are very strong and brave. They work hard and they love their families. But even in this wonderful place, sometimes nature makes things tough, like when the rain doesn't come. *Did you know?* Kenya is famous for its incredible wildlife safaris! People from all over the world come to see the animals roam free.

A Special Tree to the Rescue!

Guess what? Even when things are hard, nature often has a surprise helper! In Kenya, there is a very special tree. It's called the Gingerbread Tree! Isn't that a fun name? This tree is a true hero for many families. When there is no rain and other plants have dried up, the Gingerbread Tree keeps going. It stays strong and keeps its fruit! *Did you know?* Trees are amazing! They give us shade, clean air, and sometimes even food! Some trees can live for hundreds of years.

Why is it Called the Gingerbread Tree?

It's called the Gingerbread Tree because of its yummy fruit! The fruit looks a bit like a big sausage, but it tastes a little sweet. Some people say it tastes a bit like gingerbread cookies! Imagine that! When the people are hungry, they look for this special tree. It grows big and tall, so it is easy to spot. They carefully pick its fruit. This fruit becomes a lifesaver for many families. It gives them something to eat when other food is very hard to find.

How Does the Gingerbread Tree Help?

The fruit from the Gingerbread Tree is not just tasty, it's also good for you! People collect these fruits. They take them home and prepare them with care. Sometimes, they dry the fruit in the sun for many days. This makes it easy to keep for a long time. Then, they grind it into a fine powder. This powder is like a special flour. What do they make with this flour? They can make a kind of porridge. This porridge helps fill empty tummies. It gives energy to play and learn. It helps children stay strong. They can also mix the powder with other things to make simple bread. Imagine having a piece of Gingerbread Tree bread when you are very hungry! It's a true gift from nature. *Did you know?* Many plants have fruits or roots that people can eat to stay healthy, even if they are not common foods in your kitchen! These are called wild edible plants.

Our Friends Who Help: Oxfam

We talked about a kind group called Oxfam. These special helpers travel to places where people are struggling. They want to make sure everyone has enough to eat and drink. Oxfam and other helper groups work very hard. They try to bring food, clean water, and medicine. They also help people learn new ways to grow food even when there is little rain. They teach smart farming ideas. They are like superheroes, but instead of capes, they wear kindness and bring hope. They tell us about the problems so we can all understand and care. They want to make the world a better place for everyone. *Did you know?* There are many groups all over the world that work to help people who are in need. They are doing very important jobs, bringing smiles to faces!

Hope for the Future

Even though the drought is a big problem, there is always hope. The people of Kenya are strong. They are finding creative ways to survive, like using the special Gingerbread Tree. This shows how clever and resilient people can be. People all over the world are learning about this. They are trying to find solutions to help. We hope for more rain to come soon to Kenya and its neighbors. Rain would make the land green again and bring back many other foods. We also hope that everyone can find enough delicious food to eat every day. Thinking about our friends in Africa and what they are going through helps us understand the world better. It helps us appreciate what we have. *Did you know?* Sometimes, people build special ways to collect and save rainwater in big tanks, so they have water even when it doesn't rain for a long time! This is called rainwater harvesting.

What Can We Do?

Even little kids like you can help! How? By learning about our world and being curious. By being kind to everyone you meet. By sharing your toys and snacks with your friends. We can also draw pictures of happy rain clouds for our friends in Kenya! Or simply remember to be thankful for the food we have on our plates. Every little bit of kindness helps make the world a better place. Let's send our happy thoughts and wishes for rain to our friends in Kenya! And let's celebrate the amazing Gingerbread Tree, a true hero of nature!
summary: A special tree called the Gingerbread Tree is helping hungry children and families in Kenya find food during a time when there is not enough rain.
whyMatters: This story matters because everyone deserves to have enough food, and it shows how nature's gifts and kind people help others when times are very tough due to a lack of rain.

originalTitle: Chad warns Sudan it will retaliate after drone strike on mourners kills 17
title: Chad warns Sudan it will retaliate after drone strike on mourners kills 17
content: President Idris Mahamat Déby orders the military to be on high alert and a "total closure" of the border.

originalTitle: Denounce 'abject' Afcon decision - senior Caf member
title: Denounce 'abject' Afcon decision - senior Caf member
content: Caf executive committee member Augustin Senghor says the decision to strip Senegal of the 2025 Afcon title is "unacceptable, abject and we have to denounce it".

originalTitle: Eid under siege: Little to celebrate in Gaza as Israel tightens chokehold
title: Sad Eid in Gaza: When It's Hard to Celebrate
content:
Hello, my lovely friends across Africa and the world! It's your kid-friendly reporter here, bringing you news from far away. Today, we're going to talk about a special celebration called Eid, and why it's a bit sad for some children in a place called Gaza.

A Special Time Called Eid

Eid is a very, very special holiday for many families around the world, especially for our Muslim brothers and sisters. It's a time for big smiles, warm hugs, and lots of joy! Imagine your favourite holiday – like Christmas, Easter, or a big birthday party. That's how special Eid is! Families often get to wear brand new clothes. Everyone looks so smart and pretty! There are yummy treats to eat, like sweet cakes and delicious meals. Children usually get gifts, maybe new toys, or sometimes a little bit of money from their grown-ups to buy something fun. *Did you know?* There are actually two main Eid holidays each year! One is called Eid al-Fitr, which celebrates the end of a special time of fasting, and the other is Eid al-Adha, which is about sharing and kindness.

Our Friends in a Place Called Gaza

Now, let's talk about a place far away called Gaza. It's a land where many, many people live, and lots of children just like you call it home. Usually, during Eid, the streets of Gaza would be filled with happy sounds. Kids playing, families visiting each other, and the smell of special foods cooking. Imagine running around with your friends, showing off your new shoes, and eating your favourite sweet. That's what children in Gaza love to do on Eid, just like you might love to do on your special days. They look forward to it all year! But this year, things are very, very tough in Gaza. It’s not a happy Eid for many families there. They are finding it hard to celebrate because of big problems that make life difficult. *Did you know?* Gaza is a small piece of land near the Mediterranean Sea. It's home to almost two million people, and a very large number of them are children!

Not Enough Food, Not Enough Fun

One of the biggest problems is that there isn't enough of important things. We call this a 'shortage'. Imagine going to the market to buy bread or milk, and there is none left. That’s what a shortage feels like. Right now, many families in Gaza don't have enough food. Children are hungry, and their tummies rumble. It’s hard to play and learn when you are feeling hungry all the time. Being hungry also makes you feel very tired and sometimes sad. It’s not just food that is missing. There isn't enough medicine when people get sick. If a child gets a fever or a bad cough, it's very hard for them to get the right help to feel better. This makes grown-ups very worried. And for Eid, there aren't enough new clothes or fun toys. How can you celebrate properly if you don't have new clothes or a special treat to look forward to? It makes Eid feel very different and much less joyful for the children. *Simple explanation:* 'Goods' are things we buy, like clothes, toys, and food. 'Aid' is help, like food, water, and medicine given to people who really need it, often when there’s an emergency.

Why Is It Hard to Get Things In?

The reason for these shortages is that it's very difficult for things to get into Gaza. Imagine your house had a very tight gate, and only a tiny bit of food or water could come in each day. That’s a bit like what is happening there. There are rules and controls that make it very hard for trucks carrying food, medicine, and other helpful things to enter Gaza easily. This is what the news calls 'tighter restrictions' or a 'chokehold'. It means things are stopped or slowed down a lot. Because of these tight rules, not enough 'goods' (like everyday things we use) and 'aid' (help from other countries) can get to the families. It's like trying to fill a big bucket with a very tiny spoon – it takes a very long time, and you might never fill it up completely. This means that the shops are often empty, and even if grown-ups have a little bit of money, there might be nothing to buy. This makes life very, very hard for everyone, especially children who depend on grown-ups for everything. *Did you know?* Many good people and groups from all over the world try to send help, like food and blankets, to places where people are struggling. They want to make sure everyone has what they need.

How Do Families Feel?

When there are so many problems, families in Gaza feel very sad, worried, and tired. It’s hard for parents to see their children hungry or sick and not be able to help them easily. They might feel helpless. Children might feel confused or scared. They miss the fun and joy that Eid usually brings. Instead of laughter and games, there might be quietness and worry. It's a heavy feeling for such a special time of year. Imagine not getting your favourite meal or a new toy on your birthday because there isn't any. It would make you feel very down, wouldn't it? That’s how many children in Gaza are feeling during Eid this year.

What Can We Do?

Even though we are far away, we can still do important things. First, we can think about the children and families in Gaza. Sending them kind thoughts and hopes for a better tomorrow is a start. We can also learn about what is happening in the world and talk to our grown-ups about it. Understanding helps us to care more. And caring is a very powerful thing! We can hope that more help, like food and medicine, will be able to reach them very soon. We can hope that the rules will change so that life becomes easier for everyone in Gaza. Kindness and sharing are important lessons for all of us. *Did you know?* Even a small act of kindness, like sharing your toy or saying a nice word, can make a big difference to someone's day. Imagine if many, many people did small acts of kindness!

Hope for Tomorrow

It's sad to hear about these challenges, but we must always remember to have hope. Hope means believing that good things can happen, even when times are tough. We hope that the children in Gaza will soon have happy Eids again, with full tummies, new clothes, and lots of laughter. Thank you for listening, my dear friends. Let's send our kindness and hopes to all the children in Gaza. Until next time, stay kind and keep learning!
summary: Children and families in Gaza are having a very sad Eid because it is hard to get enough food, medicine, and toys due to tight rules on what can enter their home.
whyMatters: It matters because everyone, especially children, deserves to be safe, healthy, and happy, and to celebrate special holidays with joy, but right now, children in Gaza are not able to do that easily.

originalTitle: Iran women’s football team feted in Tehran after asylum battle at Asian Cup
title: Big Cheers! Iran's Super Girls of Football Are Home!
content:
Hello, Friends! Guess What Amazing News We Have!

Hello, hello, everyone! It’s your friendly news reporter for Africa here, bringing you super exciting stories from all over the world! Today, we have a wonderful tale about brave girls, football, and big, happy celebrations. Are you ready? Let's go!

Meet the Super Stars: Iran's Women's Football Team!

Imagine a team of girls who love to play football. They kick the ball, run fast, and score goals! These girls are from a country far away called Iran. They are super good at football, just like many of you might be! They play together as a team, working hard and chasing their dreams.

Did you know? Iran is a country in Asia, a very big continent! It has beautiful mountains and deserts. The capital city of Iran is called Tehran.

Their Big Adventure: The Asian Cup!

These amazing girls went on a big adventure! They traveled to a competition called the Asian Cup. It's like a big party where football teams from different countries in Asia come to play. They wanted to show everyone how good they were and try to win for their country.

Playing in the Asian Cup is a very big deal. All the best teams get together. Our Iran girls played their hearts out, showing everyone their amazing skills and teamwork.

Did you know? A football team usually has 11 players on the field at one time! Each player has a special job, like the goalkeeper who protects the net, or the strikers who try to score goals!

A Little Break in Australia

After playing in the big Asian Cup, something a little bit different happened. Some of the players from the Iran team needed to stay in another country for a little while. This country was Australia, which is very, very far away! They had some worries and needed a safe place to rest and think.

It can sometimes be hard to be far from home. But these strong girls looked after each other. They were brave and hoped to come back to their families and friends soon.

Did you know? Australia is a giant island country! It has unique animals like kangaroos and koalas that you won't find anywhere else in the world!

Coming Home to Big Cheers!

Guess what? The best part of the story is that they are all back! Yes, the whole team, including the players who stayed in Australia, have returned home to Iran! And everyone was SO happy to see them!

When they arrived back in their capital city, Tehran, there were big smiles, big claps, and big cheers! It was like a giant party just for them. People were so proud to welcome their football heroes home.

Imagine coming home after a long trip and seeing all your family and friends waiting for you with signs and balloons! That's how it felt for these super girls. They were "feted," which means they were celebrated and honored in a very special way!

Why is This So Special?

This is a really important story, friends. It shows us many things. First, it shows how strong and brave these girls are. They love football so much, and they never gave up on their dreams, even when things were a little bit tricky.

Second, it shows how much people love and support their heroes. The people in Iran were so happy to have their team back. They showed everyone that they care about their girls and their sport.

Did you know? Playing sports like football is great for your body and your mind! It makes you strong, helps you learn to work with others, and teaches you to never give up!

Football Connects Us All!

Even though Iran is very far from us in Africa, football connects us all! We all love watching teams play, cheering for our favorites, and dreaming of scoring goals. Football is a language that everyone understands, no matter where they live.

Just like our amazing football teams here in Africa, the Iran women's team shows us that girls can be super strong, super fast, and super good at sports! They inspire other girls and boys to play and have fun.

What Can We Learn?

This story teaches us to be brave, to work together as a team, and to never stop chasing our dreams. It shows us that even if there are some hard times, good things can happen, and we can always come home to people who love us.

So, let's give a big imaginary cheer for the Iran women's football team! They are true champions, both on and off the field. Maybe one day, some of you will be amazing football stars too!

Keep playing, keep smiling, and keep dreaming big, everyone! Until next time!
summary: The brave Iran women's football team has returned home to big celebrations after their Asian Cup adventure, showing everyone the power of courage and teamwork.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows that even when things are difficult, brave girls can achieve amazing things, inspiring everyone to play sports and never give up on their dreams, and reminding us that home is where love is.

originalTitle: Iranian football players celebrated in Tehran
title: Iranian football players celebrated in Tehran

originalTitle: Who will be England's World Cup number 10? Tuchel's selection dilemmas
title: England's Big Football Team Choices!
content:
Hello, amazing friends in Africa! It's your friendly reporter here, bringing you super exciting news from the world of football! We all love football, right? Running, kicking, scoring goals – it's the best! Today, we're talking about a very special football team from a country far away called England. They are getting ready for a huge football party called the World Cup!

What is the World Cup?

Imagine the biggest, most exciting football game ever! That is what the World Cup is! It happens only once every four years. Many, many countries from all over the world send their best football players to play against each other. It’s like a giant party where everyone plays football! Countries from Africa, like our own wonderful nations, also send their amazing teams to play in the World Cup. It's so cool to see all the different teams with their colourful jerseys and brave players. Everyone wants to win the big golden trophy! Did you know? The World Cup trophy is made of real gold! It is very shiny and heavy, and only the winning team gets to lift it high in the air! That makes it extra special to win.

Who is England?

England is a country very far from us, across the big blue ocean. They have a very famous football team, just like our favourite teams here in Africa. This team wears white jerseys and plays with a lion on their shirt. They have many talented players who kick the ball very, very well. The England team wants to win the World Cup very much. They have been practicing a lot, running fast, and trying to score many goals. They dream of bringing that shiny golden trophy back to their country. But first, they need to pick the very best players for their team.

Meet the Coach!

Every football team has a leader. Not a captain who plays, but a special person who chooses the players and tells them what to do. This person is called the coach! For the England team, the coach is a kind man named Gareth Southgate. He is a very smart man who knows a lot about football. Gareth Southgate is like the head teacher of a school, but for a football team. He watches all the players very carefully. He thinks about who is fast, who can pass the ball well, and who can score goals. His job is super important because he has to pick the team that will play in the World Cup. He needs to make sure all the players work together like a happy family. If they play together, they can be super strong! He has to choose the right players to make England the best team they can be.

Choosing the Best Players

Imagine you have many amazing friends, but you can only pick 11 of them to play a game of football with you. That's a bit like what Coach Gareth Southgate has to do! He has to pick about 26 players to go to the World Cup. But there are many, many more good players than that! He has to think about who is the fastest runner, who is the best at stopping goals, and who can score goals from far away. Every player has a special job on the field. Some players are defenders, they stop the other team from scoring. Some are midfielders, they run all over the field helping both defence and attack. And some players are strikers, their job is to score goals, goals, goals! Coach Gareth needs to pick a mix of all these super players. It's not easy because many players are very good and want to play for their country!

What is a Number 10?

Now, let's talk about a very special player on the team. This player often wears the number 10 on their jersey. This 'Number 10' player is like the magician of the team! They are usually very clever with the ball. They can pass the ball in amazing ways that no one expects. The Number 10 helps to make beautiful goals happen. They might not always score themselves, but they help their teammates score by giving them perfect passes. They are often very good at dribbling, which means running with the ball super close to their feet, past all the other players! Choosing the best Number 10 is one of the hardest choices for Coach Gareth. He has to pick someone who is super creative and can make special things happen on the field. It’s like choosing the best storyteller or artist for a big show!

Hard Choices for the Coach

Coach Gareth Southgate has to make some very big decisions. He has many good players to choose from, and they all want to play for England. Some players are very fast, some are very strong, and some are very smart with the ball. He cannot pick everyone. He has to think: "Which player will help us win the most? Who works best with the other players? Who is brave enough for the World Cup?" It’s like when you have two delicious snacks, but you can only pick one! Very hard! He needs players who are not just good at football, but also good friends on the team. They need to help each other and play with a big smile and a big heart. The coach is looking for teamwork and spirit. Did you know? A World Cup squad usually has 26 players! That means 26 players get to travel and play in the biggest football party in the world!

When Will We Know?

Soon, very soon! Coach Gareth Southgate will tell everyone his final list of players. He will announce who gets to go on the plane to the World Cup this Friday! Everyone in England, and many football fans around the world, are very excited to hear his choices. It’s like waiting to open a surprise present! We all want to see who will be chosen to wear the England jersey and represent their country on the big stage. It's a big day for football news!

Why This Matters

This is important because playing for your country in the World Cup is a dream for many, many footballers. It’s the highest honour they can get! They work their whole lives for this chance. When they are chosen, it means their hard work has paid off. For the fans, it matters because they love their team and want to see them do well. They want to cheer for their country and maybe, just maybe, see them win the World Cup trophy. It’s a time for big excitement, hopes, and dreams for everyone who loves football! It's about courage, teamwork, and the joy of the beautiful game. When the coach picks his team, he is not just picking players; he is picking dreams and hopes for a whole country. So, let's keep our eyes and ears open! We will soon know who Coach Gareth Southgate has picked for the England team. It will be an amazing World Cup, full of goals, cheers, and lots of football fun!
summary: England's football coach, Gareth Southgate, is about to announce the players who will go to the big World Cup tournament, and everyone is excited to see his important choices.
whyMatters: This matters because playing in the World Cup is a huge dream for footballers and fans want their country's team to play well and maybe even win the golden trophy!
